questions.text: A square has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: 2 diagonals and 2 lines through midpoints of opposite sides.

questions.text: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry.
questions.explanation: A circle has infinite lines of symmetry because any line passing through its center divides it into two equal halves.

questions.correctAnswer: When a figure looks the same after rotation by certain angle around its center.
questions.explanation: Rotational symmetry occurs when a figure can be rotated less than 360° and still look the same.

questions.text: The order of rotational symmetry for an equilateral triangle is ________.
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle looks the same after rotation by 120°, 240°, and 360°. So order is 3.
